title: Tetradium calcicola
taxonomy: Tetradium calcicola is a plant species within the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and molecular analysis. The plant is further classified as a genus Tetradium, which consists of several species of flowering plants. Tetradium calcicola is native to China and is often used in traditional medicine. It is a shrub or small tree that is valued for its aromatic leaves and stems.
uses: Tetradium calcicola has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its various health benefits. Its roots and stems are rich in bioactive compounds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. intro: Tetradium calcicola, also known as Chinese Evodia, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to China and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Tetradium calcicola include evodiamine, evodine, and rutaecarpine, among others, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-cancer, and neuroprotective activities. In herbal medicine, Tetradium calcicola is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) formulas to promote overall health and well-being.
